building_row.open_questions: Verify whether the target unit faces the private interior courtyard or a quieter north-facing exposure rather than Santa Monica Boulevard. Confirm the current cat fee schedule in writing, because the clearest public pet-fee terms surfaced in older unit-level Zillow listings rather than a current fee sheet. Confirm package-locker reliability, parking details, and whether the rooftop water amenity is only a hot tub.
building_row.notes: The Crown fits many of your functional needs: in-unit washer/dryer, dishwasher, AC, controlled access, key-fob entry, package service or lockers, fitness room, and rooftop work or lounge space are all publicly visible. The tradeoff is that this is an explicitly lively Santa Monica Boulevard building. The official site highlights rooftop views of the activity on Santa Monica and markets the location as vibrant and lively, which is good for walkability but not naturally aligned with a quiet-first search. There is some mitigation because current public floor-plan material references private interior-courtyard exposures and Apartments.com shows at least one available unit listed as north-facing with hill views. Management signal is workable but thin: Apartments.com currently has no renter reviews, while Birdeye aggregates a middling 3.4 from 14 reviews with both a complaint about the lack of a real pool and praise for design and location. This leaves The Crown as a verify-in-person building rather than a confident tour_candidate.

evidence_rows.claim: The official amenities page says the rooftop deck has full WiFi coverage and a bird’s-eye view of the activity on Santa Monica Boulevard.
evidence_rows.quote_or_note: Official copy frames the roof as a place to work or gather before a night out while looking directly over Santa Monica Blvd activity.

evidence_rows.claim: Official 2-bedroom floor-plan content says select units include private patios or balconies facing The Crown’s private interior courtyard.
evidence_rows.quote_or_note: This is the clearest public sign that some units may be meaningfully quieter than the boulevard-facing ones.

evidence_rows.claim: First Light Property Management is identified on current Crown site materials as the manager.
evidence_rows.quote_or_note: The page footer shows manager branding for First Light Property Management.

evidence_rows.claim: Apartments.com lists controlled access, key-fob entry, package service, hot tub, fitness center, media lounge, bicycle storage, in-unit washer/dryer, dishwasher, and air conditioning.
evidence_rows.quote_or_note: The listing also shows the property was built in 2017 with 48 units over 4 stories.

evidence_rows.claim: Resident-review evidence is mixed and thin: Apartments.com shows no renter reviews, while Birdeye aggregates a 3.4-star rating from 14 reviews with both criticism and praise.
evidence_rows.quote_or_note: Visible Birdeye snippets include a complaint that the building has only a hot tub instead of a pool and a positive comment praising design and location.

evidence_rows.claim: Current public pricing shows active concessions and deposits, but the true monthly number still depends on base-rent versus net-effective math and additional fees.
evidence_rows.quote_or_note: The listing advertises 1 month to 6 weeks free on select units, quotes $4,112 to $4,699, shows unit-specific deposits, and says displayed price may exclude mandatory fees or utilities.

evidence_rows.claim: Zillow and current unit-level listings corroborate pet-friendly status, in-unit laundry, and published pet charges, but the exact cat terms should still be re-verified on a current lease quote.
evidence_rows.quote_or_note: Visible Zillow listing text says washer and dryer are included in every unit and gives pet terms of $500 deposit per pet, $50 dog rent, and $35 cat rent.

packet_markdown:
# The Crown building packet

## Snapshot
- Building ID: the-crown-8350-santa-monica-blvd
- Address: 8350 Santa Monica Blvd, WeHo 90069
- Primary URL: https://thecrownweho.com/
- Management company: First Light Property Management
- Recommended status: research_complete
- Last updated: 2026-03-25

## Scorecard
- Quiet: 3 (low, evidence count 3)
- Management: 3 (low, evidence count 3)
- Amenity: 4 (medium, evidence count 5)
- Community stability: 3 (low, evidence count 2)
- Location fit: 5
- Pricing transparency: 3

## Risks
- Structural noise risk: medium
- Location noise risk: high
- Security risk: low
- Pricing risk: medium

## Evidence summary
- [official] The roof deck is explicitly marketed as overlooking activity on Santa Monica Blvd. Source: The Crown Amenities (https://thecrownweho.com/amenities/). Note: This is good for energy and views, not ideal for a quiet-first search.
- [official] Some two-bedroom layouts face a private interior courtyard. Source: The Crown 2 Bedroom Apartments (https://thecrownweho.com/2-bedroom-apartments/). Note: This is the strongest public quiet mitigator.
- [official] First Light Property Management is identified as manager on current site materials. Source: The Crown 1 Bedroom Townhome (https://thecrownweho.com/1-bedroom-townhome/).
- [review] Apartments.com lists controlled access, key-fob entry, package service, hot tub, fitness center, and in-unit laundry. Source: Apartments.com (https://www.apartments.com/crown-apartments-west-hollywood-ca/7p41k4z/).
- [review] Birdeye shows mixed review sentiment and a 3.4 aggregate from 14 reviews. Source: Birdeye (https://reviews.birdeye.com/the-crown-apartments-156783049108434).
- [review] Zillow unit-level text publishes washer/dryer and pet-fee terms. Source: Zillow (https://www.zillow.com/homedetails/8350-Santa-Monica-Blvd-APT-406-West-Hollywood-CA-90069/2094803695_zpid/).

## Open questions
- Is the target unit courtyard-facing or north-facing rather than directly on Santa Monica?
- Is the water amenity only a hot tub, or is there any true pool access?
- What are the exact current cat fees, parking terms, and package-locker procedures?

## Notes
The Crown has useful amenities and an appealing WeHo location, but it is publicly marketed as a lively Santa Monica Boulevard building. That makes unit orientation unusually important for your search.
